solution: 
class Solution:
    def longestPalindrome(self, s: str) -> str:
        if not s:
            return ""
        start, end = 0, 0
        n = len(s)
        for i in range(n):
            len1 = self.expand(s, i, i)
            len2 = self.expand(s, i, i + 1)
            max_len = max(len1, len2)
            if max_len > end - start:
                start = i - (max_len - 1) // 2
                end = i + max_len // 2
        return s[start:end + 1]

    def expand(self, s: str, left: int, right: int) -> int:
        while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
            left -= 1
            right += 1
        return right - left - 1
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Expand around every possible center (2n-1 centers) to find the longest palindrome in linear time per center, yielding quadratic total time with constant extra space.
